mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently yield positive equity returns, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a t-statistic greater than 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic strength and capital expenditure cycles, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP during a late-cycle expansion confirms economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative returns, with forward returns remaining positive in over 50% of recessionary quarters.
